title: tetradenia cuneifolia
intro: Tetradenia cuneifolia, also known as African boxwood, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, leathery leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, Tetradenia cuneifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
